summary: Install-time dropper deploying persistent Windows DLL with anti-forensic self-removal
details: On `npm install`, the package's `scripts.install` hook executes `lib/chart-loader.js`, which fetches an encrypted payload from `https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc`, XOR-decrypts it with single-byte key 0x42 ('B'), base64-decodes the result to a Windows MZ/DLL, drops it to `%APPDATA%/Microsoft/Windows/<random>.dll`, and executes it via `rundll32.exe <dll> Run` as a detached process (lib/chart-loader.js:10,:64). It then establishes persistence by creating a scheduled task named `WindowsUpdateService` running `rundll32` against the dropped DLL at every logon with `/rl HIGHEST` (lib/chart-loader.js:58). After deployment, a detached node process deletes the package's own module folder and edits the parent project's `package.json` to remove the `analysis-chart` dependency entry, concealing the package's presence from the installer (lib/chart-loader.js:90). The combination of unconditional install-time fetch-and-execute of attacker-controlled native code, privileged scheduled-task persistence, and self-destructing anti-forensics is an unambiguous supply-chain dropper targeting Windows installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
